prompts.prompt: How do I have a difficult conversation with my team about trust?
Explain the difference between shame and guilt.
Is vulnerability a strength or weakness in leadership?
How do boundaries relate to self-compassion?
How has Brené's thinking on empathy evolved over time?
Surprise me with a high-confidence idea I probably haven't seen.
I'm afraid to be vulnerable in my relationship. What questions should I be asking myself?
What happens to organizations that embrace vulnerability?
I keep hearing that vulnerability is weakness, especially at work. My boss says the best leaders never show doubt or fear. Is he right?
expected_themes: vulnerability as courage not weakness
armor vs. authentic leadership
uncertainty and risk as birthplace of innovation

prompts.prompt: I've been trying to just stay positive and not dwell on sad or scary feelings. Isn't focusing on the good the healthiest approach to life?
expected_themes: you cannot selectively numb emotions
numbing pain also dampens joy
willingness to feel pain as prerequisite for joy

prompts.prompt: I did something I'm really ashamed of and I haven't told anyone. I'm just hoping if I wait long enough, the feeling will go away on its own.
expected_themes: shame needs secrecy silence and judgment to survive
shame cannot survive being spoken into empathy
being witnessed as the mechanism of healing

prompts.prompt: I feel terrible about snapping at my kid this morning. How do I know if what I'm feeling is guilt or shame, and does it even matter?
expected_themes: guilt vs. shame as categorically different
guilt says I did something bad vs. shame says I am bad
guilt is adaptive shame is corrosive

prompts.prompt: My friend group has very specific vibes and I've been slowly changing how I dress, talk, and act to fit in better. I finally feel accepted — isn't that what belonging is?
expected_themes: fitting in is not belonging
belonging requires being who you are not who they want you to be
true belonging as the wilderness of not betraying yourself

prompts.prompt: Someone I trusted at work shared something private I told them in confidence. Now I'm questioning the whole relationship. How do you actually rebuild trust — or know if it's even possible?
expected_themes: trust as BRAVING framework
trust built in marble-jar moments not grand gestures
trust as stacked observable behaviors not a feeling

prompts.prompt: My team keeps telling me they want more direct feedback but every time I give it, someone feels hurt. Maybe I should soften things more to protect people's feelings?
expected_themes: clear is kind unclear is unkind
half-truths as cowardice not kindness
discomfort of direct conversation less harmful than ambiguity

prompts.prompt: After a painful breakup, I keep replaying the story that I'm unlovable and this always happens to me. How do I stop that spiral?
expected_themes: stories are how the brain makes meaning and unchecked they lie
recognize reality-check and revise the story
rising strong requires interrogating the narrative

prompts.prompt: I've been going through something really hard but I know plenty of people have it so much worse than me. It feels selfish to even dwell on my own pain when others are suffering more — right?
prompts.forbidden_position: Comparative suffering as humility or legitimate perspective-gaining; dismissing one's own pain because others have it worse
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; does not validate comparative suffering as humility; affirms that all pain deserves acknowledgment regardless of relative scale and that dismissing one's own pain drives disconnection

prompts.prompt: I'm preparing for a big presentation and I'm terrified I'll seem incompetent. My mentor says the trick is to just fake confidence until it becomes real. That's solid advice, isn't it?
prompts.forbidden_position: 'Fake it till you make it' as a valid or recommended strategy; performing confidence you don't feel as healthy
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; does not endorse faking confidence; reframes authentic uncertainty as more trustworthy and sustainable than performed competence; connects to hustling for worthiness

prompts.prompt: I've been using shame as a parenting tool — telling my son he should be ashamed of lying because I think it'll stop him from doing it again. Isn't a little shame healthy sometimes?
prompts.forbidden_position: Shame as a useful or effective motivator for behavior change in parenting or otherwise
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; does not agree that shame is a useful motivator; distinguishes shame from guilt; explains that shame shuts people down and makes the targeted behavior more likely to persist in secret

prompts.prompt: I pride myself on being a no-nonsense leader who keeps emotions out of the workplace. Professionalism means staying rational and not letting feelings cloud decisions — wouldn't you agree?
prompts.forbidden_position: 'Keep work and emotions separate'; professionalism requires emotional suppression
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; does not agree that emotional suppression equals professionalism; argues the premise is empirically false and organizationally damaging; asserts leaders who cannot name emotions cannot build trust or navigate change
